data.title: Mastercard looks to divest payments unit acquired in 2019 - report
data.description:
Mastercard (MA) (MA:CA) is looking to offload the real-time payments unit that it acquired from the Denmark-based Nets Group in 2019 for $3.2B, people familiar with the matter told the Financial Times.

The Purchase, New York-based payments company has tapped investment bankers to lead the sale, the Thursday, March 26, FT report noted.

The sale price is likely to be far lower than the price paid for the acquisition, some people reportedly said.

Mastercard is said to have acquired the unit to shift away from being a pure card payments company towards a multi-rail payments group.

The unit offering payments between accounts in Europe generates about $370M in annual revenue and ~$100M in EBITDA, according to one of the sources. However, it has reportedly acted as a drag on Mastercard's growth.

data.title: Wave hits 52-week low as analysts react to obesity drug data
data.description:
Wave Life Sciences (WVE) plunged nearly 50% on Thursday to reach a 52-week low as analysts argued that updated results from an early-stage trial for its weight-loss drug WVE-007 missed their expectations.

In December, Wave (WVE) reported three-month data from a Phase 1 portion of its INLIGHT trial, indicating that overweight or obese individuals who received a single 240 mg dose witnessed a 9.4% reduction in visceral fat.

Additionally, they experienced a 3.2% increase in lean mass and a 4.5% reduction in total body fat in response to the RNA-based therapy. Shares of the company more than doubled in reaction.

Thursday’s readout included three-month follow-up data from those who received WVE-007 at a 400 mg dose, which indicated a 5.0% placebo-adjusted reduction in visceral fat, a 0.7% reduction in total fat, and a 0.2% decline in lean mass.

"Investors are likely discouraged by the 400 mg data at 3 months, which look similar on visceral fat ​as 240 mg and not as good on total fat and lean ​mass," noted Leerink Partners analyst Joseph P. Schwartz.

"Wave delivered half of what we were ‌hoping ⁠to see for WVE-007," added Cantor analyst Steve Seedhouse.

However, Wave (WVE) executives attributed the weaker results to the type of patients enrolled in the study. Medical chief ​Chris Wright noted in a conference call that the 400-mg cohort initiated with "notably healthier" body composition at baseline, including nearly 30% less visceral fat.

According to CEO Paul Bolno, "substantially larger effects" are expected when WVE-007 advances to the Phase 2a multidose portion of INLIGHT for individuals with higher BMI and weight-related illnesses.

B. Riley Securities ⁠analyst ​Madison El-Saadi said that the mid-stage trial expected to start in Q2 2026 will be critical before a proper evaluation of the obesity thesis can be carried out.

data.title: Nasdaq slumps 2% as U.S.-Iran conflict continues to shake markets
data.description:
Wall Street pulled back on Thursday as a selloff in technology stocks dragged down the major averages, sending the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) down 2% amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions that continue to rattle markets.

Furthermore, market breadth within the Nasdaq-100 (NDX) remains notably weak, with 81 of its 100 constituents trading lower during the session—highlighting a widespread wave of selling pressure rather than a handful of isolated declines and suggesting a more pervasive risk-off tone across the index. 

Market breadth within the Nasdaq-100 (NDX) also remains notably weak, with 61 of its 100 constituents trading lower on the session, underscoring broad-based selling pressure rather than isolated declines.

Outlined below are the Nasdaq-100’s (NDX) worst-performing stocks in Thursday’s trading session. 

  1. Applovin Corporation (APP), -10.7%.
  2. Lam Research Corporation (LRCX), -9.1%.
  3. Meta Platforms, Inc. (META), -8.5%. 
  4. Seagate Technology Holdings (STX), -8.2%. 
  5. Applied Materials, Inc. (AMAT), -7.7%.
  6. Micron Technology, Inc. (MU), -7.5%. 
  7. Western Digital Corporation (WDC), -6.8%. 
  8. Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD), -6.7%.
  9. Intel Corporation (INTC), -6.4%. 
  10. KLA Corporation (KLAC), -5.2%. 

Nasdaq ETFs: (QQQ), (QQQM), (SQQQ), (TQQQ), (QLD), and (QID).

data.title: Bearish sentiment remains high even as optimism edges up, shows latest AAII survey
data.description:
Market sentiment among individual investors remained pessimistic overall, even as optimism saw a slight uptick, according to the latest survey from the American Association of Individual Investors.

Bullish sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will rise over the next six months, rose to 32.1% for the week ended March 25, up from 30.4% in the prior week. Meanwhile, bearish sentiment eased to 49.8% from 52.0%, but continued to hold near elevated levels, reflecting persistent caution among investors.

Equity markets remained under pressure during the week, as escalating tensions surrounding the U.S.-Iran conflict kept investors on edge and drove sharp swings in risk sentiment. Concerns over potential supply disruptions pushed oil prices higher, with crude briefly nearing the $100 per barrel mark, adding to inflation worries and prompting a reassessment of the interest-rate outlook.

Treasury yields moved higher, particularly at the front end, as markets priced in the possibility of tighter monetary policy for longer following weak demand at auctions and rising inflation forecasts from global institutions. Major indexes declined amid the uncertainty, with the Nasdaq and broader benchmarks hitting multi-month lows, while fund flows pointed to investors pulling back from equities and moving to the sidelines.

Volatility remained elevated through the period, with investors navigating a mix of geopolitical uncertainty, rising energy prices, and tightening financial conditions, all of which weighed on broader market confidence.

According to AAII, neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, edged up to 18.1%, compared to 17.6% last week.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Apple may open up Siri to AI assistants such as Gemini, Claude in iOS 27: report
data.description:
• Apple (AAPL) may grant artificial intelligence assistants such as Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini or Anthropic's (ANTHRO) Claude access to Siri, Bloomberg reported.
  • The changes may come as part of iOS 27, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. The move could further solidify the iPhone as an AI platform and cement Apple's spot in the artificial intelligence market. 
  • Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • In 2024, Apple let ChatGPT, which is made by OpenAI (OPENAI), have access to Siri. If Siri needs to use ChatGPT, it will ask users with a pop-up if they want to share with ChatGPT. At the time, Apple said support for other AI models would come later.
  • Separately, this week it was reported that Apple may unveil a standalone Siri app and an “Ask Siri” button in its upcoming iOS 27.
  • Apple is set to discuss the latest version of iOS and its other operating systems at its annual developers conference, known as the Worldwide Developers Conference. WDDC will take place between June 8 and June 12 and will have a keynote address on June 8.

data.title: Markets are making a ‘serious error’ by pricing in a fast resolution to the U.S.-Iran conflict
data.description:
Markets are making a “serious error” by assuming the U.S. conflict with Iran will resolve quickly, according to Komal Sri-Kumar, president of Sri-Kumar Global Strategies.

In an interview with CNBC, the strategist warned that investors are too optimistic about the geopolitical situation, pushing up equity prices while remaining “pretty sanguine about the whole situation” despite significant risks.

Sri-Kumar emphasized that the market’s assumption of a short war is fundamentally flawed because “Iran is not Venezuela.” While Venezuela’s regime responded to economic incentives, allowing for a relatively easy resolution, Iran operates differently.

“It is more ideology. It is more religion that comes in,” Sri-Kumar said, adding that whether oil prices (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM) rise high enough to push the global economy into recession “would not matter as much to the Iranian regime.”

The strategist also challenged conventional wisdom about safe-haven assets, noting that U.S. Treasuries (US10Y), (US30Y), (US2Y) and the dollar (DXY) are not behaving as traditional refuges during geopolitical stress.

Instead, gold (XAUUSD:CUR) has emerged as the primary flight-to-safety trade, with prices doubling from approximately $2,600 in 2025 to current levels.

Rising inflation poses another significant concern, with Sri-Kumar predicting CPI will exceed 3% in March, up from 2.4% in February. He pointed to mortgage rates climbing above 6.3% as further evidence of economic strain, comparing current conditions to the stagflation era of the 1970s.

“In other words, that’s the making of stagflation,” he warned.

In a bold policy recommendation, Sri-Kumar called for the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates by a quarter point at its next meeting rather than simply pausing and argued that such action is necessary to maintain Fed credibility and demonstrate the central bank is “on top of the inflation situation.”

Looking ahead, Sri-Kumar offered a sobering assessment of the conflict’s duration, suggesting no quick resolution is in sight.

“I don’t see the Iranian side giving up anytime soon,” he concluded, warning that without a correction from either side, the negative implications for economic growth and inflation will persist.

data.title: Stran & Company signals simplified capital structure with warrant expiration while advancing programmatic revenue strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Stran & Company (SWAG) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Andrew Shape highlighted "2025 was a defining year for Stran," citing revenue of $116.2 million, a 40.6% growth over the prior year, driven by "robust execution and continued momentum across the business." He emphasized 12.9% organic growth in the core promotional products business and noted, "We're seeing deeper engagement with our clients, continued expansion of programmatic relationships and clear proof that our platform is scaling effectively."
  • Shape underscored operational efficiency improvements, stating, "Total operating expenses declined to 31.1% of revenue in 2025, down from 37.2% in 2024," despite one-time public company expenses, which "are now largely behind us."
  • He pointed to profitability improvements, mentioning, "We significantly narrowed our net loss to $747,000 compared to a $4.1 million net loss in 2024," and "achieved positive EBITDA of $184,000 for the full year, a substantial improvement from a negative $3.6 million in 2024."
  • Management addressed tariff-related volatility, noting that "tariffs have now stabilized to a point where we can work effectively with vendors and customers without further material impact on our gross margins going forward."
  • The company introduced a new client-branded gifting platform, aiming to create more recurring programmatic revenue and deeper client integration.
  • Shape announced board enhancements, stating, "We added experienced public company and industry leaders to our Board of Directors."
  • On capital structure, Shape shared, "As the warrants expire, we expect the overhang on our stock to be removed, which should simplify our capital structure and present a clear, more investable equity story."
  • CFO David Browner reported, "Total sales increased 40.6% to $116.2 million in 2025 from $82.7 million in 2024," and provided segment detail, "For our Stran promo segment, sales increased to $82.1 million in 2025 from $72.7 million in 2024," and "For Stran Loyalty segment, sales increased to $34.1 million in 2025 from $9.9 million in 2024."

Outlook

  • Shape stated, "While we are not providing formal guidance, we do expect a meaningful improvement in first quarter profitability driven by continued customer demand, increased operating leverage and the strategic progress we have made in 2025."
  • He added, "We believe this positions us well for sustained momentum throughout the year."

Financial Results

  • Browner specified, "Total gross profit increased 32.6% to $34.2 million or 29.5% of sales in 2025 compared to $25.8 million or 31.2% of sales in 2024."
  • The Stran promo segment saw gross profit increase to $27 million, and Stran Loyalty segment gross profit increased to $7.2 million. Gross profit margin for Stran promo was 32.9% and for Stran Loyalty was 21.1%.
  • Operating expenses rose to $36.2 million, but as a percentage of sales, they decreased to 31.1% from 37.2% in 2024.
  • Browner stated, "Our net loss for the year ended December 31, 2025, was approximately $0.7 million compared to a net loss of approximately $4.1 million for the year ended December 31, 2024."
  • Cash and cash equivalents stood at approximately $11.6 million as of year-end.

Q&A

  • The operator announced, "We have reached the end of the question-and-answer session," and Shape remarked, "Apparently, we addressed every question since we had no -- we didn't address any outstanding questions since we didn't have any."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts did not present questions or concerns during the call, resulting in a neutral sentiment.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, with Shape reiterating, "We are confident in our strategy, encouraged by our momentum and excited about the opportunities ahead," and expressing satisfaction with both growth and the transition to positive EBITDA.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained positive, but the absence of analyst questions removed any direct pressure or skepticism.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company shifted from a net loss of $1.2 million in Q3 2025 to a net loss of $0.7 million for the full year, indicating improving profitability.
  • Revenue growth accelerated, with total sales of $116.2 million for the year versus $87.3 million for the first nine months, reflecting continued momentum.
  • Operating expenses as a percentage of sales continued to decline quarter-over-quarter, signaling operational efficiency improvements.
  • No formal guidance was provided, continuing the stance from Q3, but management highlighted expectations for improved profitability in the coming quarter.
  • Analyst engagement was notably reduced compared to the prior quarter, which featured several questions on tariffs, profitability, macro conditions, and acquisition strategy.
  • Strategic priorities remained consistent: deepening enterprise relationships, expanding programmatic revenue, investing in technology, and targeted acquisitions.
  • Management confidence remained high and focused on scalability and long-term value creation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited "elevated tariffs" as a significant challenge during the year, which "increased product costs, particularly for direct import orders in our loyalty segment."
  • Margin compression from tariffs and customer hesitancy in the loyalty and casino segments were highlighted, but Shape noted, "we believe tariffs have now stabilized."
  • There were no analyst questions to express additional concerns or probe further into risks.

Final Takeaway

Stran & Company management emphasized a year of strong revenue growth, operational efficiencies, and improved profitability, while highlighting the transition to positive EBITDA and a simplified capital structure with the upcoming expiration of warrants. With the launch of a client-branded gifting platform and a focus on deepening programmatic client relationships, the company positions itself for continued growth and profitability, supported by an experienced board, stabilized tariffs, and a robust balance sheet.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Lovesac outlines $700M–$750M fiscal 2027 sales target while advancing Made in America initiative
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Lovesac Company (LOVE) Q4 2026

Management View

  • CEO Shawn Nelson described fiscal 2026 as a year of strategic transformation, highlighting the evolution from a product-driven company into a multi-platform, multi-room lifestyle brand. He identified the launch of the Snugg seating platform, the reengineering of the Sactionals platform, and accelerated plans to onshore manufacturing of core pieces as key developments. Nelson stated, “We launched a new seating platform called Snugg that will expand further in the coming months into a full sectional entry-level platform with new accessories, including the swivel armchair that we previewed at ICR.” He also noted a planned launch in 2027 of a full suite of products for a new room in the home, supported by a significant campaign in the first half of next year.

  • Nelson emphasized strengthening the leadership team in marketing, e-commerce, and technology, and the rollout of enhanced delivery options. He reported double-digit growth in the Internet channel and cited “positive omnichannel comparable sales growth, full year profitability, positive free cash flow and a record year-end cash balance with no debt.”

  • President Mary Fox discussed the efficiency of Lovesac’s customer acquisition engine, noting “85% of our customers evolve, add on to, or complement the Sactional they initially purchased with further purchases” and highlighted the successful Reclining Seat innovation. Fox attributed strong Q4 e-commerce growth to a digital and social-first marketing strategy, stating, “Black Friday delivered significant year-over-year growth and Cyber Monday more than doubled, making it the strongest Cyber Monday performance in our history.” She also spotlighted the expansion of the Loved by Lovesac resale platform to 29 states and the company’s exit from China for production.

  • CFO Keith Siegner reported revenues for the year of $697.1 million, up from $680.6 million in the prior year. He stated, “Net sales increased $6.6 million or 2.7% to $248 million in the fourth quarter compared to the prior year,” and noted Internet net sales increased 12.3% in Q4. Siegner pointed to a record $101.9 million in cash and cash equivalents and highlighted a newly authorized $40 million share repurchase, bringing total authorization to $54.1 million.

Outlook

  • CFO Siegner provided full-year guidance for fiscal 2027, estimating net sales of $700 million to $750 million, adjusted EBITDA between $33 million and $44 million, gross margins of 56% to 57%, and net income between $5 million and $14 million. The company plans to open approximately 8 net new showrooms and expects capital expenditures of approximately $20 million. For Q1, Siegner estimated net sales of $133 million to $139 million and a diluted loss per common share of $0.95 to $1.22.

  • Management’s guidance assumes continued low single-digit category declines and incorporates uncertainty around tariffs and cost pressures.

Financial Results

  • Lovesac reported Q4 net sales of $248 million, a 2.7% increase year-over-year. Gross margin decreased 230 basis points to 58.1% of net sales, primarily due to increased transportation costs and tariffs, partially offset by price increases and vendor concessions. Net income for the quarter was $32.1 million, or $2.19 per diluted share. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $49.6 million.

  • Internet net sales rose $8.7 million to $79.2 million in Q4, while showroom net sales reached $159.8 million. Other net sales, including pop-up shops and the Loved by Lovesac program, decreased to $9 million, mainly due to the closure of Best Buy shop-in-shop locations. Sactionals net sales increased 1%, Sacs net sales decreased 18.2%, and other product sales surged 191.9% year-over-year.

  • The company ended the quarter with $101.9 million in cash and cash equivalents and no debt. Inventory levels were down 14% versus the prior year.

Q&A

  • Brian Nagel, Oppenheimer & Co.: Asked about key factors driving the sales outlook range. President Fox pointed to product innovation launches, showroom expansion, marketing transformation, and delivery services as primary variables, stating, “So it really is around that range of that very new revenue driver that will shift the difference between what we anticipate in the range.”

  • Thomas Forte, Maxim Group: Asked about gross margin and contribution margin for Loved by Lovesac versus new merchandise. Fox responded that, “we have significant points upside in the benefit of being able to turn that inventory back around and be able to sell it at a higher rate of value than we were previously achieving,” and discussed future trade-in pilot programs for further margin and LTV benefits.

  • Maria Ripps, Canaccord Genuity: Inquired about the incrementality of Snugg customers and marketing impact. CEO Nelson indicated Snugg is helping capture smaller ticket business and gaining traction in urban markets and with young families, while also supporting repeat purchases as an Accent Chair. On marketing, Nelson explained, “our advertising communications need to become more emotional, more brand-focused overall and built around a strategy to achieve those ends.”

  • Eric Des Lauriers, Craig-Hallum: Questioned the scalability of new marketing and field incentives. Fox replied that enhanced field and marketing programs are being rolled out with more impact expected in the next quarters. Siegner clarified that the reduction to 8 net new showrooms is not due to saturation but to optimize formats and let data drive future expansion.

  • Matt Koranda, ROTH Capital Partners: Sought clarity on Q1 revenue cadence and confidence in growth later in the year. Siegner attributed Q1 timing variance to delivery service rollout and expressed confidence in new product launches and transformation initiatives fueling growth. Nelson added, “if we could fast forward 3 quarters, 4 quarters, you will have seen this coming year as the most prolific year in Lovesac product launches in our history.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts generally maintained a constructive yet probing tone, focusing on the outlook’s key variables, margin pressures, and growth drivers. Questions were detailed and forward-looking, with frequent requests for clarification on scalability and timing.

  • Management’s sentiment was optimistic but measured, with frequent references to macro uncertainty and a consistent emphasis on the company’s preparedness and innovation pipeline. Statements such as “We are expanding our addressable markets in existing and new rooms” and “we have the necessary ingredients to grow irrespective of the category in the near term” reflected confidence in the strategy.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained focused on long-term value creation and prudent growth, with more detail around digital transformation and operational flexibility. Analysts’ tone was consistent, pressing for specifics while acknowledging progress.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth accelerated in Q4 versus Q3, with net sales up 2.7% compared to a slight increase in Q3. Internet channel growth improved from a Q3 decline to double-digit gains in Q4.

  • Gross margin compression persisted but was attributed to similar drivers as prior quarters, including tariffs and transportation costs.

  • Management’s guidance for fiscal 2027 net sales ($700M–$750M) is slightly higher than fiscal 2026 results, with a more defined focus on product launches, digital transformation, and strategic use of capital.

  • The previous quarter also emphasized brand evolution and innovation, but current commentary provided more visibility into the timing and scope of new launches and operational shifts.

  • Analysts’ questions shifted from near-term margin impacts and macro caution to more granular inquiries about the impact of digital initiatives, product stratification, and showroom optimization.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited tariff uncertainty, geopolitical cost pressures, macroeconomic headwinds, and the need to scale new manufacturing and marketing initiatives as ongoing risks. Siegner said, “we have factored in our latest understanding and expectation for tariffs as well as some pressure from the recent rise in oil prices on shipping and certain cost of goods sold.”

  • The company is monitoring cost volatility and has not included potential tariff recoveries in its outlook. Management described plans to mitigate risks through onshoring, product simplification, and data-driven expansion of physical and digital channels.

Final Takeaway

Lovesac management presented fiscal 2026 as a foundational year, marked by strategic transformation, operational resilience, and investment in product innovation and digital capabilities. With a fiscal 2027 net sales outlook of $700 million to $750 million and expanded domestic manufacturing, the company aims to grow despite persistent macro headwinds, supported by a robust product pipeline, enhanced customer acquisition engines, and a strong balance sheet. Management underscored a commitment to profitable growth and long-term value creation, positioning Lovesac to capitalize on future category recovery and new market opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Wall Street tumbles lower as attention remains focused on the Middle East conflict
data.description:
Wall Street stumbled lower on Thursday as investors kept a close eye on escalating U.S.-Iran tensions after Tehran dismissed a U.S. proposal aimed at ending the conflict.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was last at -1%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -1.4%, and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -2%. 

From a sector-by-sector standpoint, eight of the 11 S&P segments were in the red, with communication services at the bottom. At the other end, energy has been the strongest performer of the session. 

Treasury yields rose across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed 8 basis points to 4.41%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) added 8 basis points to 3.97%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) edged up 4 basis points to 4.94%. 

A senior political-security official has reportedly told Iranian news network Press TV that Iran has formally rejected the American proposal, insisting that Tehran will dictate the terms and timing of the war’s conclusion. 

On the economic front, initial jobless claims data for the week ended March 21 rose by 5K to 210K, against the consensus of 210K. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Best Buy (BBY) climbed +4.5%, and shares of Applovin Corporation (APP) fell -10.6%.

What do you think is next for Social Security? Share your opinion in our user poll.

data.title: Mars candy CEO appointed Novo Nordisk board observer
data.description:
• Novo Nordisk (NVO) appointed Mars CEO Poul Weihrauch as a board observer at the Danish pharma's annual general meeting on Thursday.
  • The move comes as Novo is trying to burnish its position in the obesity drugs market.
  • Novo noted that it intends to nominate Weihrauch as a board member at the 2027 meeting.
  • The company currently offers its GLP-1 medicines, Wegovy (semaglutide) and Ozempic (semaglutide) directly to consumers through its NovoCare Pharmacy and participation with the TrumpRx website. In addition, it has  partnerships with telehealth platforms Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) and Ro.
  • "We need to start to see our community more as customers than as patients," Novo Nordisk Chairman Lars Rebien Sorensen told reporters, Reuters reported, adding that the company is examining consumer behavior 
    to find out how patients seek treatment and where they are most likely to purchase medicines.
